plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-377 RP12.P04.M06.S05-RP12.P05.M03.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-377 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP12.P04 phase (M06 Synthetic Fixture Set tail, M07 Test And Golden Case Set, M08 Hermes Evidence Packet, M09 Claude Review Packet, M10 Closeout And Next Handoff) and opens the RP12.P05 foundation (M00 Scope Inventory, M01 Contract Draft, M02 Type And Shape Definition, M03 Primary Implementation Slice head) as nine descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H12), Claude review packet (C12), and CP00-378 handoff, without opening billing, proforma, invoice, or write-down runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary:
CP00-377 is a descriptor-only RP12 billing-core foundation pack (150 units, RP12.P04.M06.S05–RP12.P05.M03.S08, risk C) that closes RP12.P04 (M06 tail, M07–M10) and opens the RP12.P05 foundation (M00–M03 head). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claims are supported and the no-write/no-real-data and authority boundaries hold.

Consistency verified across all sources: registry binding (packages/billing/src/registry.js:222), contract current_pack (contracts/billing-core-contract.json:16), plan pack (docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json:412), manifest (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-377/manifest.json:1), and review baseline all agree on pack id, risk C, 150 units, range, upstream CP00-376, and next CP00-378/RP12.P05.M03.S09. Deliverable (38/13/14/39/30/10/6=150), phase (P04=92, P05=58), micro-phase, and micro-title distributions match the prompt and the REQUIREMENTS object exactly.

Implementation/tests: all seven required public exports exist and are wired through service.js, validators.js, index.js, scripts/validate-rp12-billing-core-contract.mjs, the contract projection, and packages/billing/test/model.test.js. The coverage validator enforces unit-id ordering, distribution counts, and required section rows; tests assert descriptor-only rows (descriptor_only true, runtime_execution false), evidence packets, and authority boundaries.

Production-ready claim is honestly scoped, not overclaimed: manifest production_ready=false, status implemented_pending_review, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor; command-evidence production_ready=false with status implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout. The production_ready_flag denotes descriptor verification only; production_ready_candidate=true reflects descriptor-coverage validity that the tests confirm. closeout-pack:validate / goal:closeout final-closeout commands are correctly sequenced as post-review per the hardened_review_sequence.

No-write/no-real-data boundaries are extensively attested (freezeCp377Result and both no_write_attestation copies): ~30 no-write/no-runtime flags, no_real_data, synthetic_only, loads_real_fixture_data=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false. Hermes (H12) patterns are used only for deterministic review-packet/receipt handling, not product runtime; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false.

Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false. Review-hardening (CP178+): readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid 1446-byte JSON, not 0-byte/fenced/tool-shaped/malformed); forbidden_review_evidence enumerates all required invalid-evidence rejections (failed auth, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped, missing refs, final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims); tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob; summary_only_review_allowed=false for risk C. Unrelated dirty/untracked files (workbook/*, .docx, ldip invalid-attempt) are correctly preserved out of pack scope; active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty.

No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One P3 cosmetic metadata-hygiene note in the scope descriptor (non-blocking). As a read-only reviewer I verified artifact consistency rather than re-executing the recorded validation commands; final closeout remains subject to human approval and post-review gated validation.

findings.title: Scope descriptor lists two untracked paths as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-377/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133) and in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 135-145). A path classified as pack implementation should not also be classified as unrelated; the dual categorization is semantically inconsistent. Impact is cosmetic only: active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty so no required file was dropped, and the eight active_pack_implementation_refs (the actual billing-core changes) are unaffected. Does not touch product runtime, data, or the production_ready/authority boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Make the two lists mutually exclusive, or rename unrelated_dirty_files_preserved to convey that it enumerates all preserved-but-unstaged dirty files (including pack-owned untracked docs) rather than files unrelated to the pack. Non-blocking; can be addressed in scope-descriptor tooling at convenience.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged as a non-blocking descriptor-hygiene note; does not affect CP00-377 closeout or the production_ready/no-write/authority determinations.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-377 billing core p04 closeout p05 foundation descriptor pack
